name: CARDIAC TAMPONADE
definition: PERICARDIAL TAMPONADE significant compression of heart by fluid / blood / pus / gas / tissue (= benign or malignant
distribution: simple serous fluid (underlying heart failure, renal failure, nonhemorrhagic
discriminating_features: highly specific collapse of LA during late diastole (late sign in 25%)
simple serous fluid (underlying heart failure, renal failure, nonhemorrhagic
greater than water (hemopericardium, malignancy, purulent exudates, myxedematous

CXR: normal lung fields + normal pulmonary vascularity
rapid enlargement of heart size (requires > 200 mL of pericardial effusion):
global cardiomegaly with "water bottle" cardiac silhouette
curvilinear fat density displaced posteriorly from sternum by pericardial effusion on LAT
"small heart" sign = substantial decrease in size of cardiac silhouette
RV collapse during early diastole ← pericardial pressure exceeds RV pressure (38-48%
inward movement of RA wall during late diastole + early systole lasting for > 30% of
highly specific collapse of LA during late diastole (late sign in 25%)
exaggerated respiratory variation in mitral + tricuspid inflow ← increased ventricular
distended inferior vena cava (IVC plethora) → NO decrease in diameter of proximal IVC
Doppler flow velocity paradoxus = exaggerated paradoxical increase of right versus
compression of intrapericardial pulmonary trunk + thoracic IVC → indentation of RA at
paradoxical motion of the interventricular septum → during inspiration sharply toward left,
swinging motion of heart inside pericardial sac
CT: various attenuation values:
fat (chylopericardium)
simple serous fluid (underlying heart failure, renal failure, nonhemorrhagic
greater than water (hemopericardium, malignancy, purulent exudates, myxedematous
signs of pericardial thickening:
presence of nodular areas
typically anterior location
lack of change at decubitus positioning
pericardial contrast enhancement
extrapericardial venous changes:
SVC enlargement: SVC diameter ≥ adjacent thoracic aorta
IVC enlargement: diameter > twice that of adjacent aorta
distension of hepatic + renal veins
reflux of contrast medium into azygos vein + IVC
periportal lymphedema
hepatomegaly
intrapericardial venous changes:
compression of coronary sinus
compression of pulmonary trunk
compression of short intrathoracic segment of IVC (extrapericardial posteriorly, but
"flattened heart" sign ← pericardial fluid / tissue (↑ intrapericardial pressure → transient
flattening of anterior heart surface with ↓ in AP diameter
straightening of the right cardiac contour
inversion of wall with concave deformity of RA / RV
RV + LV of equal size ← equalization of ventricular pressures
angulation / bowing / inversion of interventricular septum
MR: detection of pericardial effusions as small as 30 mL
swinging heart and paradoxical septal bounce (cine MR)
distension of IVC + hepatic veins with diastolic flow reversal (= hepatopetal flow)
episodes of high-velocity hepatopetal flow separated by long intervals of minimal flow
mass arising from / contiguous with pericardium
obliteration of normal tissue planes between paracardiac mass and heart / pericardium
compression + narrowing of cardiac chambers by pericardial effusion / mass
hyperattenuating pericardial effusion ← hemorrhage / debris with fluid usually > 20 HU
often evidence of primary malignancy of breast / lung

clinical.Rx: needle pericardiocentesis (95% success with anterior effusion > 10 mm) / surgical
